name: Sweet Rolls
summary: Soft, fluffy cinnamon sweet rolls topped with cream cheese icing.
tips: Make-ahead version: shape the rolls and refrigerate overnight before the second rise. Let come to room temp for 1–2 hours before baking.
The longer fermentation adds tangy flavor — perfectly balanced by the sweet filling and icing.
You can freeze the shaped, unbaked rolls — just thaw, rise, and bake later.

steps.title: Mix the Dough
steps.instruction: In a large bowl, mix together the starter, milk, butter, sugar, and egg until well blended. Add flour and salt, mixing until a soft dough forms.

steps.title: Knead
steps.instruction: Knead the dough for 8–10 minutes by hand or mixer until smooth and elastic. Add a touch more flour if sticky, but keep the dough soft.

steps.title: Bulk Fermentation
steps.instruction: Cover and let rise at room temperature for 6–8 hours (or overnight) until doubled in size.

steps.title: Prepare Filling
steps.instruction: Mix the soft butter, brown sugar, and cinnamon into a spreadable paste.

steps.title: Shape the Rolls
steps.instruction: Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface. Roll into a rectangle (about 9 × 13 inches). Spread the filling evenly, then roll tightly from the long edge and slice into 10–12 rolls.

steps.title: Second Rise
steps.instruction: Place rolls into a greased or parchment-lined baking pan. Cover and let rise for 2–4 hours, or until puffy.

steps.title: Bake
steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Bake the rolls for 20–25 minutes until golden brown.

steps.title: Icing (Optional)
steps.instruction: While the rolls are baking, beat the cream cheese, butter, sugar, vanilla, and milk until smooth. Spread over the warm rolls.
steps.tip: Spread icing while rolls are still warm for that gooey finish.
